calendar.matters:
Petition For Return Of Trust Property On The Bases Of Financial Elder Abuse And Undue Influence; Constructive Fraud; Breach Fiduciary Duty; Conversion
Off Calendar Feb-16-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court noted that it heard this matter back in November, that there were notice issues and sufficiency issues with this petition, that nothing has been filed since November and that it is taking that as evidence that the moving party does not intend to pursue this petition. The Petition for Return of Trust Property, etc. is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Return Of Trust Property On The Bases Of Financial Elder Abuse And Undue Influence; Constructive Fraud; Breach Fiduciary Duty; Conversion
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Continued To Feb-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Return Of Trust Property On The Bases Of Financial Elder Abuse And Undue Influence; Constructive Fraud; Breach Fiduciary Duty; Conversion
Off Calendar Nov-17-2020 Continued To Jan-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer McGuire appeared by zoom for Petitioners.
Jason Louie appeared by zoom for and with Virginia Wakefield (Objector).

The Court informed Counsel that there is a notice issue, that she needs to use form DE 115, not DE 120, that the notice has to identify the property that is to be returned to the trust and that it also needs to say that they are seeking 859 damages. Ms. McGuire stated that she is happy to correct that, and that if Mr. Louie is willing to waive it, the Court can waive it. The Court stated that there are other interested parties and that a copy of the trust is needed. Ms. McGuire stated that they initially did not have a copy of the trust, that they subpoenaed the drafting Attorney and that they now have a copy of the trust. Ms. McGuire also stated that it appears to be elder abuse and that she may have to amend her petition. The Court informed Ms. McGuire that if it is elder abuse, it needs to be filed in the Civil Department. The Petition for Return of Trust Property, etc. is continued to January 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
